title: ISic003432. Epitaph for Biktoria
body:
Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: https://www.zotero.org/groups/382445/items/Q7KT855P
1Βικτωρία
2χρηστὰ καὶ
3ἄμεμπτε χαῖ-
4ρε ἔζησε ἔτη
5κε
6ἷς ἄν τούτῳ ἐπιβου-
7[λ]εύσηται τῷ μνημίῳ δ-
8ο οὐτὸν Ὅρκος ἀπο{ο}λεῖ

l.6 reg., orig. ἷς | l.8 reg., orig. Δ Οο | reg., orig. ΟΥΤῸΝο

Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: https://www.zotero.org/groups/382445/items/Q7KT855P
1ΒΙΚΤΩΡΙΑ
2ΧΡΗΣΤΑΚΑΙ
3ΑΜΕΜΠΤΕΧΑΙ
4ΡΕΕΖΗΣΕΕΤΗ
5ΚΕ
6ΙΣΑΝΤΟΥΤΩΕΠΙΒΟΥ
7[.]ΕΥΣΗΤΑΙΤΩΜΝΗΜΙΩΔ
8ΟΟΥΤΟΝΟΡΚΟΣΑΠΟΟΛΕΙ

l.6 reg., orig. ἷς | l.8 reg., orig. Δ Οο | reg., orig. ΟΥΤῸΝο

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text of Manganaro

English translation

Jonathan Prag

Biktoria, worthy and blameless, lived 25 years. Whoever tampers with this memorial, Horkos will destroy them.

commentary

The first editors assumed that the second part of the text was perhaps written at a different time / by a different hand, but Manganaro assumes the role of a workshop in preparing the 'standard' imprecation below the tablet. In any case, the difference between the larger marble letters and the smaller letters cut onto the limestone base is already sufficient explanation for any difference. Manganaro rightly comments on the selective use of the more precious marble (this practice more familiar from statue bases). The final line is of very uncertain interpretation, appearing to show multiple errors in carving.

bibliography

  • Arias NSA 1937 467-468 no.13 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001392
  • Ferrua 1941 199-200 no.72 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001456
  • Manganaro 1962 500-501 fig.9 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001851

lunate epsilon and sigma; uncial omega.

Line 1 Interlineation line 1 to 2
